summary: A stellar 30‑minute Scarlet > Fire centerpiece drives this beloved show, with Terrapin Station and several strong covers shining on a solid recording.
review: Fans rave about the marathon Scarlet > Fire jam that still sounds fresh after decades, calling it one of the best ever. The performance also delivers memorable renditions of Terrapin Station, Dylan’s It’s All Over Now, Baby Blue, Hell in a Bucket and I Need a Miracle Every Day, while the audio quality is praised as clear enough for headphone listening despite no explicit source details.

ai_rating.rationale: The show receives overwhelmingly enthusiastic comments and high star ratings (mostly 4‑5), indicating an exceptional musical performance; recording quality is described positively but without concrete technical detail, leading to a solid but slightly lower weight.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio issues are mentioned; reviewers note the tape still sounds great after many years and works well on headphones.

standout_songs: It's All Over Now, Baby Blue
I Need a Miracle Every Day
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set revolves around an epic 30‑minute Scarlet > Fire jam that serves as the high point, with Terrapin Station and several strong covers providing momentum and emotional peaks throughout the show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Reviewer misses Jerry and emphasizes his emotional impact on the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob fans are noted to appreciate his contributions on Hell in a Bucket.
band_member_comments.Keys: One reviewer laments missing Brent (keyboardist), indicating appreciation for his playing.
